creator: klaas@kite.ping.de
text:
On Mon, Aug 31, 2009 at 11:23:36AM +0000, gavin@FreeBSD.org wrote:
> Synopsis: [usb] [hang] Kernel freezes when writing a file to an ex2fs filesystem on a usb disk
> 
> To submitter: It sounds like this may not be a USB problem, but may be
> a problem with ext2fs.  Do you still see the problem with newer versions
> of FreeBSD?  Is there any way you can verify that the same
> action on a disk not attached via USB also shows the same problem?
>
Gavin,

yes, you are correct, the problem is not limited to a disk attached via
usb. 

Since sumbitting the bug, I have gained some more experiences with it. 
It seems to appear when writing a large file to an ext2fs file system.
I believe it is a deadlock situation, created either through the way
ext2fs uses the kernel's thread locking mechanisms or in their 
implementation for the AMD64 platform - maybe even through an interaction
between both.

I upgraded to FreeBSD 7.2 about 1 month after it came out - some time
in the spring of this year, I don't remember correctly. Even with this
kernel I could lock up my system via a simple write to an ext2fs file-
system of a file larger than 2GB, e.g. (from memory):

dd if=/dev/zero of=/file/on/ext2/fs bs=1024 count=2097152

I am still running on the same system from which the bug report came,
i.e. an AMD64 dual core with a SATA2 disk. Only the memory was upgraded
to 8GB since then.

HTH,

	Klaas
